cot_content:
Okay, let's see. The question is about how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are A and B.

Option A says to tie a small knot at the speaker end of the left-hand one. Option B says to tie a knot at the connecting end of the left-hand one.

I need to think about which end would be more practical for distinguishing. Earbuds typically have two ends: the part that goes in the ear (the speaker end) and the part that connects to the wire or device (the connecting end). 

If you tie a knot at the speaker end (option A), when you put them in your ears, the knot would be inside your ear, which isn't very practical. It might be uncomfortable or not noticeable once in your ear. 

On the other hand, if you tie a knot at the connecting end (option B), that part is usually on the outside, maybe near the wire or where the earbud connects to the cable. This way, you can feel the knot when you're putting them in, helping you tell which is left or right without looking. 

So, the correct answer would be B because the knot is on the connecting end, which is easier to feel and identify when you're using the earbuds.
label: minority
